mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate elevated above 4.25% signals restrictive monetary policy that increases corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.25%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST decline by more than 8% over a 3-month rolling window, signaling weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic contraction. This negative demand shock propagates to equity valuations within 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between HOUST momentum and subsequent SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate UNRATE rises above 5.0% while payroll employment PAYEMS growth decelerates below 100k per month, indicating labor market deterioration. This joint signal of weakening labor demand reduces consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings growth, producing negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% and PAYEMS at 134789k as of 2006-02
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression models over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production INDPRO declines month-over-month while remaining above trend, indicating supply-side weakness that constrains profit margins. This supply constraint, absent demand collapse, produces negative equity returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings quality downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following INDPRO declines, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales RSAFS and real GDP GDPC1 both decelerate simultaneously—RSAFS growth below 3% annualized and GDPC1 growth trajectory flattening—signaling broad-based demand weakness. This joint demand deterioration produces negative SPX returns over 60 trading days as consensus earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 3% AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 362294M as of 2006-02, GDPC1 at 11247.6B as of 2005-10
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between RSAFS and GDPC1 momentum is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.75%, indicating monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation. This real rate compression produces positive equity returns over 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities as inflation hedges.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 199.0 as of 2006-01, FEDFUNDS at 4.49% as of 2006-02
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 48% of triggered instances, or the real rate spread (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY) shows no correlation with subsequent equity returns (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment PAYEMS growth accelerates above 150k per month while unemployment UNRATE remains stable below 5.0%, indicating robust labor market expansion. This sustained labor demand strength supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive SPX returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134789k and UNRATE at 4.8% as of 2006-02
regime_hint: tight labor market with strong job creation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to outperform a univariate PAYEMS-only model (Rank IC difference < 0.03) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production INDPRO growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while inflation CPIAUCSL remains below 2.8% year-over-year, indicating productivity-driven supply expansion without cost-push inflation. This benign supply shock produces positive SPX returns over 5 trading days as profit margins expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly change > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2.8%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.269 as of 2006-01, CPIAUCSL at 199.0 as of 2006-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between INDPRO acceleration and inflation constraint is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in any 2-year rolling window.
